name: Scaffold Cloudflare server workspace
description: Create the `server` workspace with the Cloudflare Workers baseline, Wrangler config, and the package/tooling shape needed for Workers, Durable Objects, D1, R2, Queues, and Drizzle. This task is about structure and bootstrapping only, not implementing the APIs yet.
result: Scaffolded the Cloudflare server workspace with Wrangler, Hono, Drizzle, and TypeScript. Added the baseline Worker entrypoint, Durable Object stub, D1 schema file, environment bindings, and R2/Queue/DO bindings in Wrangler config. Verified the workspace compiles with npm run typecheck under Node 24.

name: Add background processing
description: Register Android-friendly background work for screenshot syncing. When a screenshot is detected, enqueue upload work instead of uploading inline, and add periodic reconciliation scans so screenshots missed in the moment can still be picked up later.
result: Implemented the first pass of max reliability background processing. Added a native Android foreground service with a persistent notification, service-owned MediaStore observation, periodic reconciliation scans, native queue insertion into the shared SQLite database, and JavaScript controls/status for starting and stopping always-on mode.

name: Deploy backend to Cloudflare
description: Prepare the Cloudflare Worker backend for real deployment instead of local-only development. Set production-ready Wrangler config for Worker, Durable Object, D1, R2, and queue bindings; ensure migrations and deployment commands are wired; document required secrets/env setup; and verify the deployed backend can serve pairing, websocket, screenshot listing, asset reads, and upload lifecycle APIs.
result: Prepared and deployed the Cloudflare backend with production bindings, migrations, deployment docs, and working production infrastructure.

name: Scaffold web viewer workspace
description: Create the `web` workspace for the QR pairing UI and realtime screenshot gallery. Choose the baseline framework and package/tooling shape so the web app can implement pairing, websocket subscription, and gallery rendering next.
result: Scaffolded the web workspace with Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, and shadcn baseline configuration. Reduced the initial UI to a minimal hello-world shell so later pairing and gallery work can start from a clean foundation. Verified the workspace with npm run typecheck and npm run build under Node 24.

commit.message: scaffold web workspace with tailwind and shadcn base

name: Capture final touch-ups
description:
Track the final polish, UX, branding, and reliability work for Capture now that the end-to-end product is functioning. This is the main finishing bucket for the remaining improvements we want before calling the product truly polished.

Scope includes web polish, mobile UX improvements, screenshot management, branding roll-out, QR install/pair flow, synced connection state, and loading-state polish.

name: Implement screenshot upload lifecycle APIs
description: Implement screenshot init, preview completion, original completion, failure reporting, and screenshot listing routes. Support the intended lifecycle of pending, preview_ready, ready, and failed. For the current local-development phase, provide server-mediated Worker upload targets that write into the local R2 binding so end-to-end flows can be tested without remote Cloudflare infrastructure; keep the API shape swappable to direct presigned R2 uploads later. Include integration tests for the implemented routes as part of this task, not as follow-up work.
result: Implemented screenshot init, list, preview, original, and fail APIs with device/viewer auth, local Worker upload targets backed by local R2, and integration tests covering metadata lifecycle plus local binary upload storage.

name: Implement Android screenshot detection and upload queue
description: Build the Android side of the Screenshot Sync Prototype using Expo/React Native plus a small Android native module. Detect screenshots via MediaStore, keep a lightweight local queue, run background-friendly sync scheduling, upload screenshots to the backend endpoint, and expose minimal sync states such as queued, uploading, uploaded, and failed.
result: Finished the Android prototype track with screenshot detection, queueing, background processing, pairing persistence, connected-device flows, native upload execution, and real preview generation.

name: Capture post-launch follow-ups
description:
Track the next wave of product, UX, branding, and reliability work now that the deployed Capture prototype is functioning end to end. This backlog is for follow-up improvements that are intentionally deferred from the current implementation so we can pick them up one by one later without losing context.

Scope includes web polish, mobile UX improvements, screenshot management, branding roll-out, QR install/pair flow, synced connection state, and loading-state polish.

name: Build web gallery and verify end-to-end flow
description:
Build the web viewer for Screenshot Sync and verify the full end-to-end loop from QR pairing through realtime screenshot display.

Plan:
- Build a QR pairing screen that creates a pairing session and opens a websocket immediately.
- Transition the web UI from waiting state to paired state when the mobile device completes pairing.
- Render screenshot placeholders immediately from websocket `created` events.
- Swap placeholders to preview and then full images as the screenshot status updates arrive.
- Verify reconnect behavior using a REST fetch plus websocket resubscription model.

Acceptance criteria:
- Web app can pair with the phone by QR without traditional sign-in.
- Realtime screenshot UX works with placeholder-first rendering.
- End-to-end verification covers pairing, upload, preview/original state transitions, and retention/deletion sanity checks.
result: Finished the web viewer track with QR pairing, session restore, realtime gallery rendering, and deployed production web flow.

name: Sync device and client presence state
description: Add real synced connection and presence state for devices and viewer clients. Track whether each device or viewer is online, recently seen, connected, or disconnected, and make connect/disconnect state changes propagate correctly across server, web, and mobile so the product can show accurate presence instead of local-only assumptions.

name: Validate real-device behavior
description: Validate the Android prototype on a real device. Test while the app is foregrounded, backgrounded, offline and reconnecting, and confirm duplicate detection and retry behavior match expectations for the prototype.
result: Validated real-device behavior on Android, including pairing, background detection, background uploads, notifications, and deployed backend connectivity.

name: Screenshot Sync Prototype
description:
# Plan: Screenshot Sync Prototype

## Summary

Build the smallest end-to-end system that proves the core loop works:

- Android app detects new screenshots in the background and queues them for upload
- Backend accepts image uploads and stores them
- Web app shows uploaded screenshots in a simple gallery

The goal of this prototype is not perfect production reliability. The goal is to prove that background detection, upload, and viewing all work together with minimal moving parts.

## Key Changes
### Android app
- Build an Expo/React Native app with a small Android native module for screenshot detection
- Detect screenshots via `MediaStore`, not screenshot callback APIs
- Add a lightweight local queue so detected screenshots can be retried if upload fails
- Run background sync using Android-friendly scheduling, with a simple first-pass background worker
- Upload screenshots to a single backend endpoint
- Show minimal in-app sync state: queued, uploading, uploaded, failed

### Upload backend
- Create one basic HTTP upload endpoint for screenshot files plus minimal metadata
- Store uploaded images on disk for the prototype
- Return a stable URL or file id for each uploaded image
- Expose one listing endpoint so the web app can fetch uploaded screenshots

### Web app
- Build a basic gallery page that fetches uploaded screenshots and renders them newest-first
- Keep UI intentionally minimal: image grid/list, timestamp, and upload status if available
- No auth, no editing, no folders, no sharing in v1

## Build Order
1. Set up repo structure for `mobile`, `server`, and `web`
2. Implement Android screenshot detection and local queue first
3. Add upload endpoint and disk storage second
4. Connect Android uploads to backend
5. Build the simple web gallery on top of the listing endpoint
6. Test the full flow: take screenshot on Android, wait for upload, confirm it appears on the web page

## Test Plan
- Screenshot taken while app is open uploads successfully
- Screenshot taken while app is backgrounded is detected and uploaded
- Failed upload stays queued and retries later
- Server stores uploaded file and returns it in the list API
- Web app shows newly uploaded screenshots without manual data fixing
- Duplicate screenshot events do not create obvious duplicate uploads if the same file is retried

## Assumptions
- This repo is empty, so we are planning a greenfield prototype
- Android is the only mobile platform in scope for now
- Prototype storage is local disk on the backend, not S3 or cloud storage
- Prototype can skip auth and account systems
- Prototype priority is basic reliable enough to demo, not Play Store readiness or production hardening
result: Completed the Capture prototype end to end. The system now works on a deployed stack with mobile pairing, background screenshot upload, realtime web viewing, retention, and production-grade prototype flows.

name: Implement workspace websocket hub
description: Use a Durable Object per workspace to handle websocket subscription, pairing-session updates, and screenshot lifecycle fanout to all active web viewers. Keep event types minimal: pairing updated, screenshot created, screenshot updated, and screenshot deleted. Include Durable Object and Worker integration tests for the implemented behavior as part of this task.
result: Implemented a Durable Object per workspace with authenticated websocket connections, pairing and screenshot event fanout, and Worker/DO integration tests covering live event delivery.

name: Apply Capture branding assets everywhere
description: Roll the final Capture logo and brand assets across the product surface area. Cover favicon/app icons, mobile launcher icon, splash and brand surfaces, web metadata, and any remaining placeholders or old branding so the identity is consistent everywhere the product appears.

name: Build QR pairing screen and waiting state
description: Implement the web screen that requests a pairing session, renders the QR payload, stores the temporary web session token, and shows a waiting state while the mobile app scans and completes pairing.
result: Built the QR pairing screen and waiting state with restore-first viewer behavior and websocket-backed pairing updates.

name: Add debug and status UI
description:
Expand the mobile debug and status UI so development and troubleshooting remain easy as pairing, background reliability mode, and upload state become more complex.

Plan:
- Show pairing state and whether a device token exists.
- Show foreground detector status and max-reliability service status separately.
- Show queue counts and per-item status transitions including upload failures.
- Add enough debug affordances to verify that screenshots are discovered, queued, uploaded, and acknowledged by the backend.

Acceptance criteria:
- Debug UI surfaces the main states needed to reason about pairing, background detection, queueing, and upload.
- Manual testing no longer requires digging through logs for the common happy path and failure path checks.
result: Added the practical debugging and status surface needed for the prototype through lightweight app state, structured logs, native diagnostics, and upload notifications instead of a permanent heavy debug screen.

name: Add the local sync queue
description: Create a persistent local queue for detected screenshots. Track queue states such as queued, uploading, uploaded, and failed, and deduplicate using MediaStore id plus file identity so repeated scans or retries do not create duplicate uploads.
result: Implemented the persistent local sync queue in SQLite. Added queue table indexes for dedupe, queue read and summary helpers, and wired screenshot detection events to insert queued items into storage. Updated the debug UI to show real queue counts and queued screenshot rows loaded from SQLite instead of mock data.

name: Model D1 schema with Drizzle
description: Define the D1 schema and Drizzle models for workspaces, devices, pairing sessions, viewer sessions, and screenshots. Keep screenshots in a single row with preview and original storage fields. Include the indexes needed for listing, dedupe, and retention cleanup.
result: Turned the shared Drizzle schema into the real D1 model baseline. Added typed status/platform unions, device and session token uniqueness, workspace/listing indexes, screenshot dedupe uniqueness on device plus client-generated ID, and retention-friendly screenshot indexes for workspace ordering and status queries. Verified db-schema, contracts, and server typechecks under pnpm.

commit.message: model d1 schema with drizzle indexes

name: Implement web reconnect and initial fetch flow
description: Implement the web loading model that fetches the current screenshot list on load or reconnect and then resumes websocket updates. This should prevent missed events from leaving the gallery in an inconsistent state.
result: Implemented reconnect and initial fetch flow so the web gallery restores state from REST and then resumes websocket updates cleanly.

name: Implement retention cleanup policy
description: Implement the server-side cleanup rule that keeps the newest 50 screenshots or everything from the last hour, whichever set is larger. Cleanup should remove both metadata and R2 objects and emit deletion events to connected viewers. Include tests for the retention rule and cleanup selection logic as part of this task.
result: Implemented retention cleanup that keeps the newest 50 screenshots or the last hour, removes rolled-off metadata and objects, and emits deletion events.

name: Support install-or-pair QR flow
description: Make the QR entry flow handle both states cleanly: if the Captr Android app is installed, scanning should pair immediately; if it is not installed, the same QR flow should guide the user to install the app first and then continue pairing. This task is about the future install-and-continue experience and should not change the current pairing implementation until scheduled.

name: Generate real preview images
description: Generate a true smaller preview image during mobile upload instead of reusing the original bytes. Do the work in the native Android uploader so deployed latency and placeholder-to-preview behavior reflect reality. Upload a compressed preview with its own mime type, dimensions, and byte size, while keeping the original upload unchanged.
result: Generated real smaller preview images in the native Android uploader and tuned the gallery to transition from blurred preview to original image.

name: Persist paired device session
description: Store the paired device token, device id, workspace id, and configured server URL locally on the mobile app. On app launch, restore this state so a successfully paired device reopens into its connected state instead of forcing a new QR scan every time.
result: Persisted the paired device session locally so mobile restores its connected state and device auth across launches.

name: Build connected device management screen
description: Replace the scanner-only mobile flow with a paired-device screen. Show the active workspace, device id, configured server URL, pairing status, and actions to rescan or disconnect this device while keeping the dark minimal mobile theme.
result: Built the connected-device management screen with rescan/disconnect actions and the paired-state flow.

name: Polish web loading states
description: Review and improve web loading behavior across pairing, QR generation, gallery boot, refresh, and live transitions. Add or refine loaders, skeletons, and non-jarring intermediate states so the web product feels stable and intentional while data, sockets, and assets are still in flight.

name: Implement pairing and viewer bootstrap APIs
description: Implement the Worker routes for creating pairing sessions and completing them from mobile scans. Return a temporary web session token from pairing-session bootstrap, a long-lived device token from pairing completion, and wire the Durable Object so the waiting browser can react immediately when pairing succeeds.
result: Implemented POST /api/pairing/session and POST /api/pairing/complete with D1 persistence, token issuance, and workspace hub pairing notifications.

name: Polish Capture web experience
description: Improve the deployed web product experience now that the prototype is complete. Focus on UI/UX cleanup, transitions, visual polish, gallery ergonomics, copy/labels, and any small interaction fixes needed to make the web viewer feel intentional and production-ready without changing the core architecture.

name: Build paired gallery with realtime placeholder updates
description: Implement the main gallery view that listens to screenshot-created and screenshot-updated websocket events. It should render placeholders immediately, then swap to preview and full image states as assets arrive.
result: Built the connected web gallery with realtime placeholder updates, preview/original transitions, and authenticated asset rendering.

name: Bootstrap the Android app shell
description: Create the Expo-based mobile app structure for the Android prototype. Set up an Android development build path, required permissions, a minimal debug screen, and the local state or storage foundation needed to inspect screenshot detection and upload behavior during development.
result: Bootstrapped the Expo-based Android app shell for the prototype. Added the mobile workspace, Android-oriented Expo config, a debug-first home screen, SQLite bootstrap storage, and verified TypeScript compilation. A local Android native project was generated for device testing, but that generated folder remains untracked per the Expo template ignore rules.

name: Set up repo structure for mobile, server, and web
description:
Set up the remaining repo structure for the Screenshot Sync project so the Cloudflare backend and web viewer can be built cleanly next to the existing mobile app.

Plan:
- Create the `server` workspace for Cloudflare Workers, Durable Objects, D1, R2, Queues, and Drizzle-based schema/migrations.
- Create the `web` workspace for the QR pairing screen, websocket-connected gallery, and pairing/session restoration flow.
- Add any shared TypeScript contract location needed for screenshot event payloads, API request/response types, and websocket message shapes.
- Keep the repo layout simple: `mobile`, `server`, and `web` should be easy to reason about independently, with only a small shared surface for contracts.

Acceptance criteria:
- `server/` and `web/` exist with the baseline toolchain selected for implementation.
- Shared contract placement is decided and documented in the workspace structure.
- The structure supports the next backend and web tasks without needing another repo reshuffle.
result: Finished the repo structure setup for the project. Added the Cloudflare server workspace, the web viewer workspace, and a pnpm workspace monorepo layout with first-class shared db-schema and contracts packages so mobile, server, and web can share types cleanly.

commit.message: convert repo to pnpm workspace monorepo

name: Create upload backend with disk storage and listing API
description:
Build the backend for Screenshot Sync using Cloudflare infrastructure with a production-minded shape, even though the first implementation will stay minimal.

Plan:
- Use Cloudflare Workers for HTTP APIs.
- Use Durable Objects as the live workspace hub for websocket fanout and pairing-session coordination.
- Use D1 with Drizzle for persistent metadata: workspaces, devices, pairing sessions, viewer sessions, and screenshots.
- Use R2 for preview and original image storage.
- Keep the API surface tight: pairing session bootstrap/completion, screenshot init, preview upload completion, original upload completion, failure reporting, screenshot listing, and websocket subscription.
- Implement retention as a server-side cleanup rule: keep the newest 50 screenshots or everything from the last hour, whichever set is larger.

Acceptance criteria:
- Server architecture, schema, API routes, and websocket responsibilities are implemented in the repo.
- Upload lifecycle supports placeholder-first UX: `pending`, `preview_ready`, and `ready` style transitions.
- Backend can support one active mobile device and multiple viewer sessions for the same workspace.
result: Finished the backend track with pairing APIs, screenshot lifecycle, websocket fanout, retention, test coverage, and deployed Cloudflare infrastructure.

name: Verify end-to-end pairing and upload flow
description: Manually verify the prototype end to end: QR pairing, background screenshot discovery, queue upload, server lifecycle updates, web placeholder behavior, preview and original transitions, and retention sanity. Record any gaps that remain for the prototype.
result: Verified the deployed end-to-end flow across pairing, restore, screenshot upload, pending-to-preview-to-ready transitions, and deployed web/backend connectivity.

name: Fix multiple image copy in web gallery
description: Make multi-selection copy in the web gallery behave honestly and usefully. Right now toolbar copy is misleading because browsers do not support Finder-style multi-file image clipboard behavior in the same way native desktop apps do. Decide on the right product behavior for one item vs many items, implement it, and update the UI so copy and any fallback actions (such as download or zip later) match platform reality.

name: Refactor mobile app into components
description: Split the mobile app out of App.tsx into focused pairing, connected-device, and shared UI components before the upload client work. Create a clearer mobile structure with separate screen components, shared UI primitives, and pairing/session hooks or helpers so the next upload work does not pile more logic into a single file.
result: Refactored the mobile app into focused pairing, connected-device, and shared UI components instead of keeping logic in App.tsx.

name: Set up Cloudflare Workers test harness
description: Set up server testing with Vitest and @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers so Worker routes, Durable Objects, and D1-backed flows can be tested inside the Cloudflare runtime. Add initial tests covering the backend work already implemented so far, including pairing-session creation, invalid pairing completion, and successful pairing completion with workspace-hub notification state.
result: Set up Vitest with @cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers, D1 test bootstrap, and pairing route integration tests covering create, invalid completion, and successful completion flows.

name: Build connected devices list screen
description: Change the paired mobile state from a single-device detail card into a connected-devices style screen. Show the current connected device as a list item, keep the dark Zen theme, and preserve actions for scanning a new QR code or disconnecting before the upload-client task begins.
result: Changed the paired mobile state into a connected-devices style screen with a simpler scanner/list flow.

name: Implement upload client
description:
Build the Android upload client that takes locally queued screenshots and pushes them through the server upload lifecycle.

Plan:
- Add mobile support for pairing completion and long-lived device-token storage.
- For each queued screenshot, call the server init endpoint first to create the screenshot record and obtain upload targets.
- Start original upload immediately.
- Generate and upload the preview in parallel so the web app can move from placeholder to blurred preview quickly.
- Call the server preview and original completion endpoints as each asset finishes.
- Mark queue items as uploaded or failed, and retry failures with simple backoff.

Acceptance criteria:
- Mobile can authenticate as a paired device and upload queued screenshots to the backend.
- Queue state transitions are reflected locally for success and failure.
- Upload flow supports the intended realtime UX without blocking original upload on preview generation.
result: Implemented the mobile upload client end to end, then moved the effective background upload path into native Android so pending, preview, and original uploads work off the app lifecycle.

name: Add screenshot viewing and management on mobile
description: Extend the mobile app beyond pairing/device management so users can also browse and manage synced screenshots from the phone. Define the minimal useful mobile screenshot experience, including viewing, browsing, and future management actions that fit the Capture product direction.

name: Implement real screenshot deletion flow
description: Turn the current placeholder delete UI into a real end-to-end screenshot deletion flow. Wire web gallery delete actions through the backend, update screenshot state and retention behavior consistently, remove deleted items from live gallery state, and ensure mobile/web/client views stay in sync when a screenshot is removed.

name: Define shared API and event contracts
description: Add or choose a shared contracts location for API request/response shapes and websocket event payloads used across mobile, server, and web. Keep this layer intentionally small and focused on the pairing and screenshot lifecycle. Use a pnpm workspace monorepo so shared packages are first-class packages instead of path aliases into app internals. Extract Drizzle schema into packages/db-schema and transport contracts into packages/contracts, then rewire server, web, and mobile to consume those packages cleanly.
result: Defined the shared API and websocket contract layer as a pnpm workspace package and converted the repo into a proper pnpm workspace monorepo. Extracted Drizzle schema into packages/db-schema, extracted transport contracts into packages/contracts, rewired server/web/mobile to consume workspace packages, and verified workspace typechecks and web build under pnpm.

commit.message: convert repo to pnpm workspace monorepo

name: Build screenshot detection
description: Add a small Android native module in Kotlin to detect new screenshots through MediaStore. Normalize detected screenshot metadata into a JS-friendly shape including id, uri, filename, timestamp, and relative path, and filter aggressively so only likely screenshots become sync candidates.
result: Implemented Android screenshot detection with a custom Kotlin native module registered in the Expo/React Native app. The detector watches MediaStore image changes, queries recent images, filters likely screenshots using name/path/recency heuristics, and emits screenshot candidate events to JavaScript. Added a JS wrapper and debug UI controls to start and stop the detector and display detected screenshot candidates on device.

name: Polish mobile app UX
description: Review and improve the mobile app UX now that the core pairing and upload pipeline works. Focus on rough edges in scanner flow, connected-device presentation, copy, spacing, polish, and any onboarding or device-management interactions that still feel prototype-grade.

name: Deploy backend to Cloudflare
description: Prepare the Cloudflare Worker backend for real deployment instead of local-only development. Set production-ready Wrangler config for Worker, Durable Object, D1, R2, and queue bindings; ensure migrations and deployment commands are wired; document required secrets/env setup; and verify the deployed backend can serve pairing, websocket, screenshot listing, asset reads, and upload lifecycle APIs.
result: Deployed the backend to Cloudflare with working D1, R2, Durable Objects, queue bindings, production health checks, and production CORS.
